query:
Both findings are correctly classified. Neither blocks merge.

  Finding 1 is the more meaningful one from a maintenance perspective. The ordering invariant      
  (SupportsContradict before Informs in merged_pairs) is load-bearing — a future reordering in
  Phase 6 could silently cause Phase 8b to miss Informs variants entirely, with no test failure and
   no error. The comment fix is one line and costs nothing. This should be done before the retro,
  not filed as a future issue.

  Finding 2 is belt-and-suspenders. The input constraints make non-finite weight practically       
  impossible — nli_informs_cosine_floor is validated at startup to be in (0.0, 1.0), and
  nli_informs_ppr_weight to [0.0, 1.0], so the product of two valid finite f32s is always finite.  
  The debug_assert is correct documentation of the invariant. The production guard adds one
  continue branch that will never fire. Low value but zero cost — reasonable to include.

  Disposition: both can be handled as a single retro commit before tagging the PR as merged. One   
  comment, one guard. No re-review needed. Perform fixes prior to merge pls

entries.title: merge_configs: per-file validation does not cover cross-level invariant violations; always re-validate the merged result
Batch structural changes before compiling — multi-file struct extension and bugfixes both benefit from complete-then-compile discipline
ADR-001: NliCandidatePair Tagged Union for Merged NLI Batch (corrected)
ADR-001 (crt-035): Bidirectional Tick Updates Are Eventually Consistent, Not Atomic Per Pair
Verify reported bug scope is still live before diagnosing: gates can be silently removed and components can be removed from pipelines
